overview:
## Discussion

The `CodeResources` file that gets generated as part of the code signing process serves as the bundle’s seal. This file is a CFDictionary that contains a listing of all the files found within your bundle coupled with their respective hash values and a set of rule definitions. The type of object returned depends on which item in the dictionary had a problem. See [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/technotes/tn2206/_index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/DTS40007919] for more information on the `CodeResources` file.

# kSecCFErrorResourceSeal

**Global Variable**

A key whose value is a Core Foundation object containing the part of the resource seal that had a problem.

## Declaration

```swift
let kSecCFErrorResourceSeal: CFString
```

## Discussion

The `CodeResources` file that gets generated as part of the code signing process serves as the bundle’s seal. This file is a CFDictionary that contains a listing of all the files found within your bundle coupled with their respective hash values and a set of rule definitions. The type of object returned depends on which item in the dictionary had a problem. See [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/technotes/tn2206/_index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/DTS40007919] for more information on the `CodeResources` file.
title: kSecCFErrorResourceSeal
